messages.subject: Vectrix evaluation — Meridian Health Systems
messages.body_text:
Hi Priya,

I'm Alan Fong, VP of Engineering at Meridian Health Systems. We found Vectrix through a Hacker News thread earlier this week and I've been exploring the platform.

We run 85+ production APIs supporting clinical data workflows. We currently use a combination of Datadog and in-house tooling for API monitoring, and neither gives us the endpoint-level anomaly detection we need.

Before I escalate this internally, I want to understand four things:
1. Data residency — we require all data to stay in US-based infrastructure.
2. SSO — we're on Okta and need SAML integration.
3. Retention — our compliance team requires 12-month data retention.
4. SOC 2 status.

If those four are addressable, I'd like to set up a call.

— Dr. Alan Fong
VP Engineering, Meridian Health Systems
alan.fong@meridianhealth.com

Dr. Alan Fong
VP Engineering, Meridian Health Systems
alan.fong@meridianhealth.com

messages.subject: Re: Vectrix evaluation — Meridian Health Systems
messages.body_text:
Dr. Fong,

Thank you for reaching out — and those are exactly the right questions.

Quick answers:
1. Data residency: All data stored and processed in AWS us-east-1 and us-west-2 (US only). No data leaves the US.
2. SSO: We're mid-migration to Clerk, which supports SAML 2.0 and Okta integration natively. We can have SSO configured for your org within 30 days of contract.
3. 12-month retention: Our standard is 90-day raw events. We support custom retention contracts for Enterprise — per-org retention config is in development (target March 1).
4. SOC 2: We hold Type I (issued October 2025). Type II audit committed for Q2 2026.

Tessa Moreno (CC'd), our Head of Sales, will reach out to coordinate a call.

— Priya Chandran
CEO, Vectrix Labs
priya@vectrix.io

messages.subject: Vectrix Security Questionnaire — responses and SOW draft attached
messages.body_text:
Dear Rachel,

Thank you for sending over Meridian's vendor security questionnaire. I'm attaching our initial responses and a copy of the SOW draft for your legal team's review.

We've completed approximately 60% of the questionnaire; the remaining questions (primarily SOC 2 controls evidence and detailed encryption specifics) will be completed by February 14.

A few items I want to flag proactively:
- Q47 (SLA Credits): Our standard policy caps credits at 30% of monthly fee. Priya is reviewing options and we expect to address this in the SOW redline.
- Q61 (ClickHouse subprocessor): Our analytics database runs on ClickHouse Cloud, which uses AWS us-east-1 as its underlying infrastructure.
- Q3 (SOC 2 Type II): We hold Type I. Type II audit committed for June 30, 2026.

Please let me know if you have questions.

— Nina Kowalski
Ops & People, Vectrix Labs
nina@vectrix.io

messages.subject: Re: Vectrix Security Questionnaire — structured questionnaire attached
messages.body_text:
Hi Nina,

Thank you for these. A few comments after reviewing the initial responses.

We've prepared a structured questionnaire (attached as .xlsx) with all 147 items — please use this format for your complete responses rather than the free-form document, as it maps to our vendor approval system.

Key items flagged by our security team:
- Q47: The 30% SLA credit cap does not meet our vendor standard for PHI-adjacent infrastructure. We require a minimum of 100% of monthly fee or uncapped. This needs to be resolved in the SOW before legal review can proceed.
- Q61: Please confirm ClickHouse Cloud's underlying cloud provider and region explicitly in your response.
- Q3: We are provisionally accepting the Type II commitment with a contractual backstop (termination right if not achieved by Sept 30, 2026).

Please return the completed .xlsx by February 14.

— Rachel Simmons
VP IT Security, Meridian Health Systems
rachel.simmons@meridianhealth.com
